title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs security, privacy, reliability, and layered quality evidence from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for trust and quality assurance case after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Threat Abuse Case
steps.instruction:
Order the Threat Abuse Case evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Threat Abuse Case.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Threat Abuse Case assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Threat Abuse Case, keep Threat Abuse Case connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Threat Abuse Case has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

stimulus.title: Quality Nonclaim case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. Artifact under review: trust and quality assurance case.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Quality Nonclaim, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Quality Nonclaim

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Threat Abuse Case to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Threat Abuse Case.
